company_core.name: Periodic Labs
company_core.description: The main product consists of AI scientists and autonomous laboratories designed to conduct experiments that generate high-quality data, particularly in the physical sciences. This technology utilizes advanced AI methodologies to improve materials design, with specific applications in superconductors and semiconductor manufacturing.

founders.name: Ekin Dogus Cubuk
founders.background:
Ekin Dogus Cubuk served as a Research Scientist at Google DeepMind from 2017 to 2025, where he contributed to advanced AI research. Prior to that, he was a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at Stanford University from 2016 to 2017. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Physics and Engineering from Swarthmore College and a Master of Arts in Condensed Matter and Materials Physics from Harvard University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/ekin-dogus-cubuk-9148b8114

founders.name: William (Liam) Fedus
founders.background:
William (Liam) Fedus has a strong background in AI and deep learning, having served as a Research Scientist at Google Brain from 2020 to 2022, where he focused on deep learning research. He was a key contributor to the development of ChatGPT while working at OpenAI, first as a Member of Technical Staff and later as VP of Research for Post-Training. Liam holds a PhD in Computer Science from Université de Montréal, where he studied under notable figures like Hugo Larochelle and Yoshua Bengio, and also earned a Master's degree in Elementary Particle Physics from UC San Diego.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/william-liam-fedus-26547811

agent_output.markdown:
# Periodic Labs | Meeting Brief
*2026-03-03*

## TL;DR
Periodic Labs is a newly founded (2025) AI-for-science company building autonomous laboratories and AI scientists to accelerate physical sciences research, with a focus on materials design, superconductors, and semiconductor manufacturing. Despite being at seed stage, the company has raised a remarkable $300M from a top-tier syndicate including a16z, Lightspeed, and Bain Capital Ventures. The founder pedigree — a Google DeepMind research scientist and a former OpenAI VP of Research who contributed to ChatGPT — is exceptional and likely explains the outsized raise.

---

## Company Overview
Periodic Labs develops AI scientists and autonomous laboratory systems designed to run experiments and generate high-quality data in the physical sciences. Core applications include materials design, superconductors, and semiconductor manufacturing. The company operates B2B and sits at the intersection of AI, advanced manufacturing, and robotics. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

- **Founded:** 2025 [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
- **Stage:** Seed [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
- **Headcount:** 39, up 44.4% in the last 90 days [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)[[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
- **Web Traffic:** 35,580 visits, down 17.4% in the last 30 days [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)[[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
- **Total Raised:** $300M, closed September 30, 2025 [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

---

## Key Points
• **Extraordinary seed raise for the stage:** $300M at seed is highly anomalous and signals extreme investor conviction — or a redefined notion of "seed." This warrants clarification on how capital will be deployed and what milestones define the next stage. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

• **Elite founder credentials:** Co-founder Ekin Dogus Cubuk spent 2017–2025 as a Research Scientist at Google DeepMind with a background in condensed matter and materials physics — directly relevant to the product thesis. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)[[3]](https://swarm.com)

• **High-profile AI pedigree on the other side:** Co-founder William (Liam) Fedus was VP of Research for Post-Training at OpenAI and a key contributor to ChatGPT, with prior deep learning research at Google Brain. His PhD lineage under Yoshua Bengio and Hugo Larochelle at Université de Montréal is top-tier. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)[[3]](https://swarm.com)

• **Rapid team scaling:** 44.4% headcount growth in 90 days (from a base of ~27 to 39) suggests aggressive hiring post-funding close, consistent with a fresh large raise. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

• **Top-tier investor syndicate:** Andreessen Horowitz, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Bain Capital Ventures, Felicis, and Quiet Capital are all on the cap table. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

• **Declining web traffic is a flag:** A 17.4% drop in traffic over 30 days at this early stage could reflect limited public-facing product activity, or it may simply indicate the company is pre-launch and not yet driving inbound. Worth probing. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

---

## Recent Developments
• Closed a $300M seed round on September 30, 2025, backed by a16z, Lightspeed, BCV, Felicis, and Quiet Capital. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
• Headcount has grown 44.4% over the past 90 days, indicating active team buildout post-close. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
• No additional historical news data available yet — tracking initiated from first retrieval. [[1]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

---

## Signals & Trends
• **Hiring surge** consistent with deploying fresh capital; team growing rapidly from a small base. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
• **Web traffic declining** (-17.4% over 30 days) — likely pre-product-launch, but worth monitoring as a potential indicator of limited market awareness or early traction challenges. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
• **Seed label on a $300M round** is unusual; competitive dynamics in AI-for-science (autonomous labs, materials discovery) may be driving investors to move fast and large. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)

---

## For This Meeting

**Key questions to ask:**
- Why is this labeled a seed round at $300M — is this a single tranche or structured with milestones? What does the deployment timeline look like?
- What is the current state of the product — are autonomous labs operational, in pilot, or still in development?
- Who are the first target customers, and is there any early commercial traction or LOIs in semiconductor/materials sectors?
- How do the two co-founders divide responsibilities, and are there other key hires (e.g., a CTO, Head of Science)?

**Risks to probe:**
- **Capital efficiency:** $300M at seed with 39 people implies an enormous runway — or very capital-intensive lab infrastructure. Understand the burn model.
- **Hardware dependency:** Autonomous physical laboratories require significant physical infrastructure. What is the build vs. partner strategy?
- **Declining traffic:** Is this a pre-launch company with no public product, or is there a go-to-market gap forming early?

**Areas needing clarification:**
- Titles and role split between Cubuk and Fedus are not specified in available data. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
- No customer names, pilots, or revenue data available. [[1]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
- The specific AI methodologies underpinning the platform are not detailed in available data. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/35555342)
